fixed: Low (900px): long bracket-placeholder titles ('Round of 16 5 Winner – Round of 16 6 Winner') clipped mid-word with an ellipsis while the same text wrapped in full on phones. Made .ev-title wrap at all widths (overflow-wrap: break-word) instead of nowrap+ellipsis, so the full name always reads.
notes: The three high-severity 'Norway – England' duplicate-row findings are a DATA problem, not a rendering bug, so they are out of scope for ui-fix (which may only touch the frontend). events.json holds two separate records for the same match: 'England at Norway' (static ESPN, no source) and 'Norge – England' (source: ai-research), both sport=football, time=2026-07-11T21:00, homeTeam=Norway/awayTeam=England. build-events.js dedupes on 'sport|title|time' and misses them because the title strings differ; both then render identically via homeTeam/awayTeam. The durable fix belongs in the data layer (build-events dedup on match identity incl. teams) — left for self-repair / the pipeline, deliberately NOT band-aided in the client.

CSS fix verified with before/after screenshots at 375/393/900px: the ellipsis clip is gone at 900px and placeholder titles wrap to a full second line; 375/393 unchanged (they already wrapped); no new overflow/truncation; layout stays a calm single column with header/agenda/'Hva vi følger'/footer intact.
